steps.title: Find the original median
steps.content: With $51$ stamps, the median is the $26$th value. The cumulative count reaches $24$ by age $76$ and $28$ by age $81$, so the $26$th value is $81$.

steps.title: Check the new median
steps.content: After adding one stamp, there are $52$ stamps, so the median is the average of the $26$th and $27$th values. Both are still $81$, so the median stays $81$.

steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content: The new stamp is $136$ years old, which is greater than every age in the original table. Adding a value above the original distribution increases the mean while leaving the median unchanged. The answer is B.
